purpose: Validate concrete on-device Form-native execution lanes after the form-llama local model validation exposed that strict Form-native runtime is still separate from Ollama/MLX.

receipt: Form compiled the code, emitted the Mach-O object, ld linked it, and the binary ran.
mac.boundary: No clang compiler or assembler in the emitted object path. macOS ld remains the linker carrier.

receipt: file: ELF 64-bit LSB shared object, ARM aarch64, dynamically linked, stripped
The Android linker dlopen'd the Form-emitted .so and ran form_native(7,6,5) on device.
android.boundary: The .so was emitted by Form without clang. NDK clang built only the tiny dlopen test harness, not the native Form artifact.

windows.object_floor_command: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/language-model.fk form-stdlib/form-pe-coff.fk form-stdlib/tests/form-pe-coff-band.fk form-stdlib/tests/form-pe-coff-arm64-band.fk
windows.boundary: PE/COFF x64 and arm64 object floors are proven four-way. The standalone Windows form-cli runtime proof requires running the PowerShell script on a Windows host.

form_proofs.name: form-macho-band
form_proofs.command: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/form-asm.fk form-stdlib/form-lower.fk form-stdlib/form-macho.fk form-stdlib/tests/form-macho-band.fk

form_proofs.name: form-pe-coff-band + form-pe-coff-arm64-band
form_proofs.command: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/language-model.fk form-stdlib/form-pe-coff.fk form-stdlib/tests/form-pe-coff-band.fk form-stdlib/tests/form-pe-coff-arm64-band.fk

verdict.notes: This validates Form-native code execution on Mac and Android, plus Windows object emission. It does not yet validate form-llama inference/training as Form model cells or a Form-native model runtime.
